title: unclear documentation on podman pod ps
body:
In [the docs for podman pod ps](https://docs.podman.io/en/stable/markdown/podman-pod-ps.1.html), it is not made clear what the command does with no arguments.

The synopsis states that it "lists the pods on the system", which could technically refer to only running pods or all pods on the system.
The examples state that it both [lists only running pods](https://docs.podman.io/en/stable/markdown/podman-pod-ps.1.html#:~:text=List%20all,2) and [lists all current pods](https://docs.podman.io/en/stable/markdown/podman-pod-ps.1.html#:~:text=List%20all%20current,3,-Filter).

Can somebody clarify which of these it shows and update the docs accordingly?

comments.body: `podman pod ps` shows all the containers, and you're right, it'd be clearer if the doc says " lists _all_ the pods" instead. Interested in raising a PR for this?
@danishprakash By 'all containers' do you mean all pods?

yes, tbc, `podman pod ps` shows all pods.
while `podman ps` only shows running containers by default
